chats.text: For the record — I will be physically absent from this room for a brief period to address fiscal reporting applications. Please see me at your earliest convenience.
Tax day is this Wednesday? Holy sh*t, my golf reservation was for Tuesday night 😭
{NAME}, can we loop on the cognitive overhead allocation during fiscal reporting? The downstream impact on day-to-day execution is a total delta. Please advise.
I told HR I'd be at the mini-golf tournament in lieu of this meeting. They said 'enjoy.' I'm bringing a spreadsheet.
The impending fiscal reporting applications create a temporary reallocation of mental bandwidth. We need to anticipate any corresponding short-term productivity disruption.
Ryan, are you actually going to do the tax work or just send me a slide deck titled 'The Process'? I need the actual numbers for Q3 attainment.
Charles — historical speaking, my workflow is aligned to fiscal reporting cadence. The deliverables are prepared.
If you're not here, how can I allow the org around minimizing any corresponding total short-term productivity disruption?
I'm literally at the putting green. You know the drill — I've been historically speaking very committed to golfing for team morale.
Let me close that loop real quick. The tax-based deliverables may create cognitive overhead amongst personnel.
If anyone else is golfing this week, let's grab drinks afterward 🍬
The downstream impacts of a modest increase in finance-related external focus can affect day-to-day execution. Please mitigate.
I just realized my tax return was filed under the wrong name and I'm not sure how to fix that without going through a lawyer
That being said, if anyone needs me during this period — please feel free to contact my assistant. I will be available via email.
So if you're 'available via email' during fiscal reporting, does that mean I should just send you my golf scorecard and call it done?
Right. That’s the baseline posture going forward.
So I’ll lock the golf plan for post-tax day, right?
{NAME}, remember what you saw in that deck—Ryan’s absence is a delta, not just a time block. The org needs to treat it as a systemic headwind.
I’m happy to answer any questions before we move forward right?
You want me to send the golf update? I’ll call it ‘Team Time Prevalence’—the finance team already bought that one.
If anyone has blockers on their immediate deliverables, let’s flag them now, right?
You said ‘minimal disruption’ but the team’s been asked to do more with less since you last joined. The math doesn’t work.
Great, thank you all for being so proactive here,
{NAME}, if you see Ryan’s name on a deliverable next week it’ll probably be ghosted. I mean that professionally. He’s off the grid.
Charles, your golf plan is fine as long as it doesn’t conflict with any team-wide initiatives right?
{NAME}, I saw you liked that ‘cognitive overhead’ phrase. Want to borrow the slide? I’ll swap in a graph so it looks like real data.
We’ll circle back next week with any necessary adjustments, right?
I'll be in touch with you directly regarding the downstream impact assessment right?

Hey, did you get that? I told Ryan he's 're-calibrating' but like—historically speaking—he just hates scheduling around my golf tee times. Also FYI the org is calling it a 'productivity delta' so don't tell anyone I said anything right?
Your 'team time prevalence' is historically low. I need your full bandwidth during the Q3 attainment window. Right?
Your absence creates downstream headwinds. I'm already planning a 'cognitive deceleration' plan. Historically speaking, you're the bottleneck right?
Also I just found out our finance apps are scheduled for Wed 10 AM. Does anyone else have a 9-5 right?
I need you to buffer the team's cognitive overhead from the impending fiscal reporting. Your 'bandwidth reallocation' will be measured in Q3 attainment right?
Please minimize any corresponding total short-term productivity disruption. Thanks.
FYI Ryan just sent me a calendar invite titled 'Midway Tax-Based Deliverables' for a meeting that doesn't exist. I'm calling it 'attainment shortfall' until someone says otherwise right?
I'll be unavailable for the duration of the application window. Please document all downstream impacts in my shared folder.

I just saw Ryan's calendar. His 'tax day' block says 'Meditation & Corporate Synergy'—but his assistant texted me it was just him reorganizing his spice rack right? Historically speaking he does this every quarter.
